2604.06871.title: Do We Need Distinct Representations for Every Speech Token? Unveiling and Exploiting Redundancy in Large Speech Language Models
2604.06871.paper_summary: Large Speech Language Models (LSLMs) typically operate at high token rates (tokens/s) to ensure acoustic fidelity, yet this results in sequence lengths that far exceed the underlying semantic content, incurring prohibitive inference costs. In this paper, we empirically revisit the necessity of such granular token-level processing. Through layer-wise oracle interventions, we unveil a structured redundancy hierarchy: while shallow layers encode essential acoustic details, deep layers exhibit extreme redundancy, allowing for aggressive compression. Motivated by these findings, we introduce Affinity Pooling, a training-free, similarity-based token merging mechanism. By strategically applying this method at both input and deep layers, we effectively compress speech representations without compromising semantic information. Extensive evaluations across three tasks demonstrate that our approach reduces prefilling FLOPs by 27.48\% while maintaining competitive accuracy. Practical deployment further confirms significant efficiency gains, yielding up to $\sim$1.7$\times$ memory savings and $\sim$1.1$\times$ faster time-to-first-token on long utterances. Our results challenge the necessity of fully distinct token representations, providing new perspectives on LSLM efficiency.
2604.06871.paper_summary_zh: 大型語音語言模型（LSLMs）通常以高標記速率（tokens/s）運作，以確保聲學的真實性，然而這導致序列長度遠超過基礎語義內容，從而產生高昂的推理成本。本文中，我們實證性地重新檢視這種細粒度標記級處理的必要性。通過層級的預言者介入，我們揭示了一個結構化的冗餘層級：雖然淺層編碼了基本的聲學細節，但深層則顯示出極端的冗餘，允許進行激進的壓縮。受到這些發現的啟發，我們引入了親和力池化（Affinity Pooling），這是一種無需訓練的、基於相似性的標記合併機制。通過在輸入層和深層策略性地應用這種方法，我們有效地壓縮了語音表示，而不損害語義信息。在三個任務上的廣泛評估顯示，我們的方法將預填充的FLOPs降低了27.48\%，同時保持了競爭性的準確性。實際部署進一步確認了顯著的效率提升，實現了高達$\sim$1.7$\times$的記憶體節省和$\sim$1.1$\times$的長語句首次標記時間加快。我們的結果挑戰了完全獨立標記表示的必要性，為LSLM效率提供了新的視角。
2604.06871.author: Bajian Xiang et.al.
2604.06871.authors: Bajian Xiang, Tingwei Guo, Xuan Chen, Yang Han
